ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302050 covers mochi as a food product category. No specific entry for assorted filled mochi set with Hokkaido cream and chocolate fillings. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EKX covers rice cake as a broad category. No specific code for Japanese filled mochi (daifuku) with cream or chocolate filling. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for mochi or Japanese rice cake confectionery. None assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a set of 6 mochi pieces, each approximately 40 g, totalling approximately 240 g, as sold by Kazo Premium at Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mochi (Japanese rice cake with sweet filling) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite estimate for filled mochi confectionery
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Kazo Premium Hokkaido and chocolate filled mochi exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for mochi (glutinous rice cake) and filled daifuku analogues (USDA FDC NDB 45226358 and related entries), weighted for a 50% plain mochi shell and 50% sweet cream/chocolate filling composition. Energy, macros, and secondary nutrients are derived from this composite estimation. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mochi / glutinous rice cake analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kazo Premium filled mochi. GI of 61 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for glutinous rice-based confectionery and mochi analogues (GI range 55–65 reported for glutinous rice products). A midpoint of 61 is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 × 129.6) / 100 ≈ 79.1, rounded to 79.0. Serving carbohydrate = 54.0 g per 100 g × 240 g serving = 129.6 g.

hypertension.guidance: With approximately 38 mg sodium per 100 g, a 6-piece mochi set contributes about 91 mg sodium per serving, which is low and unlikely to adversely affect blood pressure in hypertensive individuals when consumed occasionally.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate and sugar load with medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: A 6-piece serving delivers approximately 130 g of carbohydrate and 67 g of sugar with a glycemic load of approximately 79, making this a high-glycemic-load food that should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 6-piece serving provides approximately 9.8 g of saturated fat and 67 g of sugar; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this high-sugar, moderate-saturated-fat confectionery to occasional treats only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Kazu Kazu Assorted Mochi Set (6 pieces) is a premium Japanese confectionery product sold by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall, Singapore. Each set contains six individually crafted mochi pieces: three Hokkaido-style mochi filled with rich Hokkaido milk cream, and three chocolate mochi filled with chocolate cream or ganache. Mochi is made from glutinous rice flour (mochiko) that is steamed or pounded into a soft, chewy, slightly sticky dough, then wrapped around a sweet filling. The texture is characteristically soft, elastic, and pillowy on the outside, with a smooth, creamy or fudgy interior depending on the filling. The Hokkaido variant has a milky, lightly sweet flavour profile, while the chocolate variant is richer and more indulgent. Nutritionally, this set is energy-dense at approximately 285 kcal per 100 g, driven primarily by carbohydrates (54 g per 100 g) and sugars (28 g per 100 g), with moderate fat (6.8 g per 100 g) from the dairy-based fillings. A full 6-piece serving (approximately 240 g) delivers around 684 kcal, 130 g carbohydrate, and 67 g sugar, making it a high-glycemic-load food (GL ≈ 79). Clinically, this product is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high sugar and carbohydrate content. It should be consumed as an occasional treat by those managing cardiovascular disease or weight. The product is vegetarian but not vegan due to dairy-based fillings, and is not certified halal. It contains milk allergens and possible soy and gluten cross-contamination.
common_user_queries: What is in the Kazu Kazu mochi set?
How many calories in 6 pieces of mochi?
Where to buy premium mochi in Singapore?
How much sugar is in mochi?
What is the glycemic index of mochi?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Kazo Premium Hokkaido and chocolate filled mochi exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, or ICMR-NIN. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central mochi and filled daifuku analogues using a composite shell-to-filling ratio model. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database glutinous rice confectionery analogues. Confidence is medium due to estimation from analogous sources rather than direct measurement.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central mochi and filled daifuku analogues; no exact branded entry for Kazo Premium products was found in any approved primary source. 2. Serving size of 240 g is estimated at 6 pieces × 40 g each based on typical premium mochi piece weight; actual piece weight may vary. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogue source entries used. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogue source entries used. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the analogue source entries used. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogue source entries used. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogue source entries used. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogue source entries used. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as glutinous rice confectionery with dairy and chocolate fillings contains no meaningful vitamin C per USDA analogue data. 10. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database glutinous rice confectionery range (55–65); no direct measurement for this product exists. 11. Halal status is unconfirmed; Kazo Premium is a Japanese-origin brand and halal certification is not stated on the GrabFood listing. 12. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style '6pcs Kazu Kazu Set (Assorted)' to the canonical form 'Kazu Kazu Assorted Mochi Set (6 pieces)'. 13. Cuisine is classified as Japanese as mochi is a Japanese confectionery; the product is sold in Singapore. Multilingual names include all five mandatory Singaporean languages plus Japanese.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values are documented in notes. Confirmed vitamin_c_mg 0.0 is documented as confirmed zero. Verified ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Confirmed GI > 0 with numeric value. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass.
